message:
fix(planarity): is_planar accepts G= kwarg for nx-parity (br-sl3mn)

The raw Rust binding exposed ``is_planar(g)`` — lowercase parameter
name — which broke keyword-argument calls like ``is_planar(G=g)``
that upstream nx supports. Wrap in Python with the nx signature
``is_planar(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs)``; delegate to
the Rust binding for the actual planarity check. The same
_validate_backend_dispatch_keywords guard used elsewhere rejects
stray backend kwargs.

Regression tests added to TestPlanarity: keyword-arg call and
rejection of unexpected kwargs.

Suite: 3941 passed (was 3939) / 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -4755,9 +4755,21 @@ def dag_longest_path_length(G, weight="weight", default_weight=1):
 )
 
 # Planarity
-from franken_networkx._fnx import is_planar
+from franken_networkx._fnx import is_planar as _raw_is_planar
 from franken_networkx._fnx import is_chordal
 
+
+def is_planar(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs):
+    """Return True if ``G`` is planar.
+
+    Matches upstream ``nx.is_planar(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs)``
+    signature so keyword calls like ``is_planar(G=g)`` work
+    (franken_networkx-sl3mn); the raw Rust binding used ``g`` as the
+    parameter name which broke kwarg parity.
+    """
+    _validate_backend_dispatch_keywords("is_planar", backend, backend_kwargs)
+    return _raw_is_planar(G)
+
 # Barycenter
 from franken_networkx._fnx import barycenter

@@ -191,6 +191,18 @@ def test_non_planar_graph(self):
         G = fnx.complete_graph(5)
         assert not fnx.is_planar(G)
 
+    def test_is_planar_accepts_G_kwarg(self):
+        """Regression guard for franken_networkx-sl3mn — the parameter
+        must be named ``G`` (matching upstream nx) so callers can use
+        keyword-argument form."""
+        G = fnx.complete_graph(4)
+        assert fnx.is_planar(G=G) is True
+
+    def test_is_planar_rejects_invalid_backend_kwargs(self):
+        G = fnx.complete_graph(4)
+        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
+            fnx.is_planar(G, bogus_kwarg="x")
+
     def test_is_planar_transformed_graphs_match_networkx(self):
         planar_self_loop = fnx.cycle_graph(4)
         expected_planar_self_loop = nx.cycle_graph(4)
